**Paolo Ferrero** (born 17 November 1960) is an Italian politician. He is a leading member of the [Communist Refoundation Party](/wiki/Communist_Refoundation_Party "Communist Refoundation Party") (*Partito della Rifondazione Comunista*; PRC), and served as Minister of Social Solidarity from 2006 to 2008 as part of the [Prodi II Cabinet](/wiki/Prodi_II_Cabinet "Prodi II Cabinet").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paolo_Ferrero&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Ferrero was born in [Pomaretto](/wiki/Pomaretto "Pomaretto"), in the [province of Turin](/wiki/Province_of_Turin "Province of Turin").

A former [FIAT](/wiki/FIAT "FIAT") worker, his political commitment began at the age of 17, when he joined [Proletarian Democracy](/wiki/Proletarian_Democracy "Proletarian Democracy") (*Democrazia Proletaria*; DP). Unlike the majority of his party, Ferrero is religious and is a member of the [Waldensian Evangelical Church](/wiki/Waldensian_Evangelical_Church "Waldensian Evangelical Church"), and was the leader of the youth evangelical Waldensian federation before fully entering politics in 1987. He served as a [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin") city councillor from 1993 to 1997, shortly after the DP was absorbed by the PRC. He has two children.

Ferrero was first elected as a Member of Parliament in 2006. Prior to being appointed to Prime Minister [Romano Prodi](/wiki/Romano_Prodi "Romano Prodi")'s Cabinet in 2006, he was responsible for the PRC's social, economic and labour department, with a particular focus on social policy, national civil service, non-EU immigrants and drug-related policies.

On 27 July 2008, Ferrero was elected secretary of the PRC, defeating the other major candidate, [Nichi Vendola](/wiki/Nichi_Vendola "Nichi Vendola"). His term of office came to an end in April 2017.
